questions.prompt: Your chat product feels sluggish — there's a 3–5 second silent gap before every response appears. What's the simplest fix?

choices.text: Switch to a smaller, faster model.

Enable streaming. The model produces tokens as it generates them — first token typically arrives in 200–600ms, even if the total response takes 5 seconds. Massive perceived-latency improvement with no other code changes.

questions.explanation: Streaming is the standard fix for perceived latency. Same model, same answer, completely different UX — users see the response materializing within ~half a second. Always stream for user-facing responses.

questions.prompt: Your product has a 'deep research' operation that takes 4–6 minutes. Holding users on a loading screen, completion rate is 18% — most leave. What's the right architecture?

choices.text: Keep the loading screen but make it more entertaining.

Switch to background mode + webhook. User submits request, gets immediate acknowledgement. API runs research in the background and POSTs results to your webhook when complete. Notify the user (email, push, in-app) when ready. Completion rate typically jumps to >90%.
Reduce the operation to under 30 seconds even if quality drops.
Make the user re-submit the request every 30 seconds.

questions.explanation: Long-running operations don't fit synchronous UX. Background mode + webhook is the standard pattern: acknowledge quickly, run async, notify on completion. The user can do something else while the research runs. Holding users on a loading screen past ~60 seconds destroys engagement.
